pr.title: Why hire me?
pr.author: 0ce10tsgit
pr.subtitle: wants you to merge his skills into your team

paragraphs: Introduction
My name is Oyon (Oy-in) and I am an undergrad currently pursuing a B.S in Information Technology and Web Sciences at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute. This is my PR to get you to hopefully hire me.
Cloud Deployment Skills

To begin with, this site is one of many applications I've developed, deployed and maintained myself. I learned cloud deployment both through this site with Digital Ocean and through my ITWS1100 class with Azure cloud deployment for a term project. I also converted my home PC to a dual boot web server that hosts AI models that I'm training and can access through my network
Data Skills
I have experience with PostgreSQL and MariaDB, both designing schemas as well as writing a lot of SQL which I learned from my ITWS1100 class and through personal projects
In other Data skills I took a Biology Computational Lab where I used R on Datasets to prove/disprove a hypothesis
I've also trained AI models using Hugging Face and Python with Google Colab to create a chatbot that talks like me based on my texts
Backend Skills
I have experience building backend systems with FastAPI, PHP, and Node.js.
Specifically I've made two REST APIs for both an open contribution cookbook app and a ride sharing website.
Languages I know overview
I've got experience in a number of languages
For starters, I took and passed Data Structures and Algorithms in C/C++. The 10 hr/week homework demanded optimized, quality code.
I have college credit in Java through AP Computer Science A as well as experience with it in FTC robotics
I'm deeply experienced in Python and JavaScript/TypeScript in a number of scenarios including backend, automation and AI training
I also have an unhealthy obsession with Google Apps Script. It's fantastic for automation and everyone should learn it.

commits.message: A nifty NPM package style script I wrote when learning Node

paragraphs: Python

One of my side projects is creating an LLM that talks like me, which I am doing using Hugging Face and Python. It trains on my texts, which are converted into training data by another Python script I wrote to extract and organize them into conversations in a set format.
Another past project was writing a compiler I consider near Turing complete. I was able to make it compile and run a BrainF*ck interpreter within it (an odd programming language, look it up).
I've also used it in web development, building a FastAPI REST API for a carpooling app.
A number of Discord bots I've made use discord.py. One notable one relays data from Google Sheets into a Discord server.
Use of AI in development
I believe that AI is a powerful and useful tool when used in the correct scenarios and ways
I follow the basic principle of 'if you can't explain how it works, don't use it.' That rarely comes up because I do iterative, conversational development.

With skills you have knowledge of, it is deeply helpful in increasing productivity as well as being good for debugging. When in unknown territory it is a fantastic learning tool. If you don't understand what it did, talk to it until you do

I'm proud to have retained knowledge of how to do things without AI as well as practicing those skills often (academics), but I have experience with workflows in Claude Code that I find have me shipping faster
This website is actually one of the places I use Claude Code in development to help me learn better frontend
Conclusion

I hope this PR has convinced you to hire me. I have done a lot and intend to do a lot more, and I definitely haven't covered all of it here. If I am an applicant, you have my contact info. I am very open to discussing my skills in the context of what you need and would love to talk.
Thanks for reading
